TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi abstract sealed beforefieldinit AcquireTokenOptionsExtensions extends System.Object

Docs.summary: SDK-to-SDK helpers for flowing the outgoing to a request-aware authorization header provider through .
Docs.remarks: Not meant to be used by application developers, but by other SDKs. Some protocols bind the authorization header to the outgoing request - for example a PoP SignedHttpRequest's q/h/b claims hash the request's query, headers, and body. The calling SDK sets the request before the header is created; the header-provider SDK reads it. Using the existing bag avoids adding a new interface for each request-bound protocol (SHR, mTLS PoP, acquisition metadata, ...).

MemberSignature.Value: .method public static hidebysig class System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage GetHttpRequestMessage(class Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.AcquireTokenOptions options) cil managed
<Extension()> Public Function GetHttpRequestMessage (options As AcquireTokenOptions) As HttpRequestMessage

Docs.param: The token acquisition options to read the request from.
Docs.summary: Gets the outgoing previously stored by , or if none was set. The reader does not own the request; the SDK that created it remains responsible for disposal.

MemberSignature.Value: public static void SetHttpRequestMessage (this Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.AcquireTokenOptions options, System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage httpRequestMessage);
.method public static hidebysig void SetHttpRequestMessage(class Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.AcquireTokenOptions options, class System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage httpRequestMessage) cil managed
<Extension()> Public Sub SetHttpRequestMessage (options As AcquireTokenOptions, httpRequestMessage As HttpRequestMessage)

Docs.param: The token acquisition options to attach the request to.
The outgoing request whose material may be bound into the header.
Docs.summary: Stores the outgoing so a request-aware authorization header provider can bind to it. The request's content should already be set so that body binding is accurate.
Docs.remarks: Writes to a fresh copy of . shallow-copies the dictionary reference, so a per-call options object can share one instance with a DI-singleton; cloning before writing keeps this per-request value isolated to the current call and prevents concurrent acquisitions from observing each other's request.
